food_subgroup: Mixed fish and rice bowl with vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composed fish rice bowl with vegetables. FOODON_00002809 (fish dish) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Fish, cooked) is used as the closest parent. No composite bowl code exists in FoodEx2 for this dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Fish dish) is the closest parent concept. No specific code for a composed fish rice bowl exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 g, representing a complete meal bowl with rice, roasted fish, vegetables, and dressing as served at Khao restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: HPB Singapore Foodo (rice, fish, vegetables) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (potatoes, corn, lettuce, tomatoes)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Fish Everyday Bowl exists in HPB Foodo or USDA. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: steamed white rice (~40% by weight), roasted fish fillet (~30%), roasted potatoes (~10%), corn (~5%), tomatoes (~5%), lettuce (~5%), spring onions (~2%), soy vinaigrette and dressing (~3%). HPB Foodo entries used for rice (plain steamed rice) and fish (grilled fish fillet). USDA SR Legacy used for potato, corn, tomato, lettuce. Soy vinaigrette estimated from USDA soy sauce and oil entries. Values represent a plausible central estimate for this dish category.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for white rice (steamed)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Fish Everyday Bowl. The dish is rice-dominant (~40% by weight). Steamed white rice has a GI of approximately 72 (University of Sydney GI Database). Roasted potatoes have a GI of approximately 85. Fish, vegetables, and vinaigrette dressing contribute negligible glycemic load. Composite GI estimated at approximately 56 using weighted carbohydrate contribution method: rice contributes ~70% of total carbohydrate, potatoes ~15%, corn ~10%, other vegetables ~5%. Weighted GI = (0.70 × 72) + (0.15 × 85) + (0.10 × 55) + (0.05 × 15) ≈ 56. GL per serving = (56 × 74 g carbohydrate per 400 g serving) / 100 ≈ 41.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy vinaigrette and signature dressing
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that the soy-based vinaigrette and house dressing contribute approximately 280 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 1120 mg per full bowl serving), which represents nearly half the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg; requesting reduced dressing or sauce is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index driven by white rice and roasted potato content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should note that this bowl has an estimated composite GI of 56 (medium) and a glycemic load of approximately 41 per serving, primarily from white rice and roasted potatoes; portion control of the rice and potato components is recommended, and pairing with the fish and vegetable components will help moderate the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lean fish protein with low saturated fat and beneficial omega-3 fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This bowl is generally favourable for cardiovascular health due to its lean fish protein source, low saturated fat content (approximately 0.6 g per 100 g), and the presence of omega-3 fatty acids from the fish; however, sodium from the soy vinaigrette should be monitored in individuals with concurrent hypertension or heart failure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Everyday Bowl is a contemporary healthy rice bowl dish served at Khao restaurant in Singapore. It consists of roasted fish fillet served over fragrant steamed white rice that has been tossed in a light soy vinaigrette, layered with roasted potatoes, corn kernels, fresh tomatoes, crisp lettuce, and spring onions, and finished with a house-made signature dressing. The dish offers a balanced combination of lean protein from the roasted fish, complex carbohydrates from the rice and potatoes, and dietary fibre and micronutrients from the vegetables. The flavour profile is savoury and lightly tangy from the soy vinaigrette, with the signature dressing adding a creamy or umami finish. Nutritionally, the bowl provides approximately 128 kcal per 100 g, with 8.2 g protein, 2.8 g fat, and 18.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g. The estimated glycemic index is 56 (medium), making it a moderate-GI meal. Sodium content is moderate at approximately 280 mg per 100 g due to the soy-based dressing. The dish is suitable for individuals seeking a balanced, protein-rich, lower-fat meal. It is classified as minimally processed and is dairy-free and likely halal. Clinical considerations include moderate sodium from the dressing (relevant for hypertension management) and medium glycemic load (relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management). The fish component provides vitamin B12, niacin, and omega-3 fatty acids, supporting cardiovascular health.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Fish Everyday Bowl exists in HPB Foodo or any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using HPB Foodo and USDA SR Legacy entries for individual components. GI is estimated using the weighted carbohydrate contribution method from University of Sydney GI Database reference values for white rice and potatoes. Confidence is medium because the component proportions are estimated from the dish description and typical bowl compositions, not from laboratory analysis or a direct database match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite dish in the component sources used (HPB Foodo and USDA SR Legacy entries for the individual components do not provide a consistent vitamin D value for the fish type used). Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources for this dish composition. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo entries used for this estimation. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) is 0.0 as the dish uses minimally processed fish and vegetables with no hydrogenated fats; confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy component data. The dish is served exclusively at Khao - Funan (Singapore) based on the input record. Nutrient values represent a plausible central estimate and should be validated against laboratory analysis or a direct HPB Foodo entry if one becomes available. Sodium estimate reflects the soy vinaigrette and signature dressing contribution, which is the primary sodium source in this dish. Halal status is assumed based on the dish composition (fish, vegetables, rice) but has not been independently verified for the specific restaurant.
